vision: PRPs-Framework is an IDE-agnostic context engineering toolkit that turns AI-assisted software development into a structured, traceable, JSON-driven workflow.

target_audience.primary: SA/BA and software developers who use AI coding agents and need a structured, repeatable workflow for requirements, planning, implementation, and verification.
secondary: Technical leads standardizing AI-assisted delivery across teams
Solo developers who want stronger context management for agentic coding
AI IDE power users working across Cursor, Windsurf, and Antigravity
QA and review specialists who need traceable task artifacts

phases.name: Foundation / MVP Hardening
phases.description: Make the framework reliable for workspace-based roadmap and task workflows by adding canonical files, validation, and documentation hygiene.

milestones.title: Canonical project context is available
milestones.description: Required project index and roadmap discovery artifacts exist in the expected .workspaces locations and validate successfully.

milestones.title: Documentation renders cleanly
milestones.description: Thai and English documentation renders as UTF-8 without mojibake and explains the core workflow clearly.

phases.name: Workflow Reliability
phases.description: Strengthen day-to-day developer use with root-level validation, reproducible sync, and clearer onboarding.

milestones.title: One command validates framework health
milestones.description: Maintainers can run a documented root-level command to verify schemas, scripts, dashboard assets, and required artifacts.

milestones.title: IDE bundles stay in sync
milestones.description: Cursor and Antigravity framework trees can be compared or regenerated from a single source of truth.

phases.name: Adoption and Scale
phases.description: Improve adoption across teams with guided setup, roadmap governance, and richer dashboard feedback.

milestones.title: New users can onboard quickly
milestones.description: A concise quickstart and health check help users install, activate, and verify the framework in a target project.

milestones.title: Roadmap operations are visible and governed
milestones.description: The project has an explicit roadmap, release cadence, and dashboard indicators for artifact health.

features.title: Generate canonical project_index.json
features.description: Create and maintain .workspaces/project_index.json using the workspace project index shape. Include detected services, key directories, entry points, dependencies, infrastructure, and conventions.
features.rationale: The roadmap and planning agents expect project_index.json, and missing context causes downstream phases to fail or infer from incomplete information.
acceptance_criteria: Given the repository root, when project indexing runs, then .workspaces/project_index.json is created as valid JSON
The index contains services for the root framework and IDE-specific bundles
The roadmap output directory receives a project_index.json copy usable by roadmap_features.md
user_stories: As a planning agent, I want a canonical project index so that I can reason about services and conventions without re-scanning the whole repository
As a maintainer, I want required context files generated predictably so that roadmap generation does not fail at startup

features.title: Formalize roadmap discovery output location
features.description: Standardize roadmap discovery files under .workspaces/roadmap and document when root-level copies are convenience outputs versus canonical runner outputs.
features.rationale: Users and agents currently may look in different directories, which creates confusion between manually generated files and runner-managed roadmap artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: roadmap_discovery.json exists in .workspaces/roadmap and validates against the discovery prompt contract
Documentation names .workspaces/roadmap as the canonical roadmap output directory
Root-level discovery files are clearly marked as optional human-readable or convenience artifacts
user_stories: As a roadmap generator, I want discovery data in the expected output directory so that feature generation can proceed without path ambiguity
As a developer, I want to know which files are canonical so that I do not edit the wrong artifact

features.title: Normalize documentation encoding
features.description: Convert README, SETUP, AGENTS, and related Thai/English docs to clean UTF-8 rendering. Add a lightweight check that catches mojibake in critical documentation files.
features.rationale: Thai-language adoption is a differentiator for this framework, and garbled documentation undermines trust and onboarding quality.
acceptance_criteria: Thai text and symbols render correctly in README.md, SETUP.md, and AGENTS.md
A documented validation step checks critical docs for common mojibake patterns
No workflow instructions are lost during encoding normalization
user_stories: As a Thai-speaking team member, I want documentation to render correctly so that I can follow setup and workflow guidance without guesswork
As a maintainer, I want encoding issues detected early so that docs stay readable after updates

features.title: Add root-level framework validation command
features.description: Expose a documented root command that validates JSON schemas, required .workspaces artifacts, CLI entrypoints, script tests, and dashboard asset presence.
features.rationale: The framework already has validation scripts inside IDE bundles, but users need one obvious command to confirm the repository is healthy.
acceptance_criteria: A root-level command validates required JSON artifacts and reports missing files with actionable messages
The command verifies root package scripts and the .agent package entrypoint are present
The command exits non-zero when required roadmap or schema files are invalid
user_stories: As a maintainer, I want one validation command so that I can check framework health before release
As a user installing the framework, I want a clear pass/fail check so that I know setup is complete

features.title: Create agent bundle health checker
features.description: Add tooling to verify the .agent bundle has required agents, rules, schemas, scripts, skills, and dashboard assets, while confirming legacy bundle directories are absent.
features.rationale: Duplicated IDE trees are useful for compatibility but create maintenance risk unless drift is visible and intentional.
acceptance_criteria: The checker reports missing required .agent files and forbidden legacy bundle directories
Intentional differences can be allowlisted
The root validation command includes the sync check or links to it
user_stories: As a framework maintainer, I want to detect IDE bundle drift so that Cursor and Antigravity users receive equivalent workflows
As a contributor, I want clear drift reports so that I know which bundle needs updating

features.title: Introduce single-source bundle generation
features.description: Design a source-template or manifest-driven process that can regenerate IDE-specific folders from shared content while preserving necessary IDE differences.
features.rationale: A sync checker finds drift, but generation from one source reduces long-term duplication cost as the agent and command library grows.
acceptance_criteria: A shared manifest identifies files copied into each IDE bundle
Generation preserves Cursor, Windsurf, and Antigravity-specific names or paths
Generated output can be compared against checked-in bundles without unexpected changes
user_stories: As a maintainer, I want shared framework content generated into IDE-specific layouts so that updates are less error-prone
As a contributor, I want one place to edit shared agent guidance so that I do not duplicate changes manually

features.title: Build a guided quickstart and health check
features.description: Create a short onboarding path that walks users through installation, active IDE selection, symlink or copy setup, validation, and first task creation.
features.rationale: The framework has powerful workflows, but new users need a fast path to success before they learn every command and persona.
acceptance_criteria: A quickstart guide gets a new user from clone to validated setup in under 10 minutes
The guide covers both copy-based and symlink-based installation
The health check confirms active IDE files, project index, roadmap directory, and dashboard assets
user_stories: As a new user, I want a concise quickstart so that I can try the framework without reading every document first
As a team lead, I want repeatable setup steps so that team members configure projects consistently

features.title: Publish human-readable roadmap and governance docs
features.description: Add ROADMAP.md and lightweight governance guidance covering release cadence, artifact ownership, roadmap review, and how generated roadmap JSON maps to human planning docs.
features.rationale: The project needs a visible strategic direction so users can understand what is planned, what is stable, and how to contribute improvements.
acceptance_criteria: ROADMAP.md summarizes phases, priorities, and near-term focus
Governance notes define who owns generated artifacts and how updates are reviewed
The document links to .workspaces/roadmap/roadmap.json as the machine-readable source
user_stories: As a stakeholder, I want a readable roadmap so that I can understand project direction without reading JSON
As a maintainer, I want governance notes so that generated artifacts do not become stale or conflicting

features.title: Add dashboard artifact health indicators
features.description: Extend the PRPs dashboard to surface missing or stale workflow artifacts such as project_index.json, roadmap_discovery.json, roadmap.json, requirements, plans, and QA reports.
features.rationale: Users benefit when framework health and task traceability are visible, not hidden in terminal logs or folder checks.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard displays whether required project and roadmap artifacts exist
Dashboard distinguishes missing, stale, invalid, and healthy artifact states
Users can open or locate the relevant artifact from the dashboard context
user_stories: As a QA reviewer, I want dashboard health indicators so that I can quickly see whether task artifacts are complete
As a developer, I want missing files surfaced visually so that I can fix workflow setup before running agents
